scope: The engine gains a single role-dispatch Driver contract (internal/driver: Driver/RoleSet/DispatchInput/Result + AssertWorktree) that an incapable driver is rejected against at resolution time, never mid-run — contract package and ADR only, no driver implementation, no orchestrator rewiring.

reachability.evidence: go test ./internal/driver/... -v: TestRoleSet (membership, empty set, String() fixed-order naming, single-role String()) PASS; TestAssertWorktree (plain checkout success, linked-worktree success where .git is a file not a directory, missing-path failure, non-directory failure, non-git-directory failure) PASS; TestNoWireImports (AST-parses every .go file in the package, fails naming any internal/model or internal/agent import) PASS. Full-repo go test ./... green (44 packages, no regressions) confirming the new package doesn't disturb anything importing internal/driver's future consumers.

delivered.item: AC-01: Driver/RoleSet/DispatchInput/Result types with the exact field shape decided 2026-07-02
AC-02: RoleSet over {implementer, verifier, captain} with Has(Role) and deterministic String()
AC-03: godoc + ADR state VerdictSchema carries the schema, driver returns StructuredJSON, engine validates fail-closed, driver never self-certifies
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/driver.go (Driver.Dispatch godoc, DispatchInput.VerdictSchema godoc); docs/adr/0012-driver-contract.md 'Engine-owned verdict validation'

delivered.item: AC-04: AssertWorktree fail-closed on missing path / non-directory / not-a-git-worktree, naming the path and failed check; success on both a plain checkout and a linked worktree (.git as a file)
AC-05: internal/driver imports neither internal/model nor internal/agent
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/imports_test.go TestNoWireImports (AST-parses all .go files in the package)

delivered.item: AC-06: ADR-0012 records the Type-1 decision (options, decider, four clauses); go build ./... and go test ./internal/driver/... pass

delivered.evidence: design.md line ~77 rephrased without a specific issue number; docs/adr/0012-driver-contract.md never cites sworn#35

delivered.item: Design-review pin 2 (AssertWorktree must handle linked worktrees, .git as a file)
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/worktree.go uses git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree, not a directory-presence stat check; driver_test.go 'success: linked worktree' subtest exercises it against a real git worktree add checkout

delivered.item: Design-review pin 3 (confirm DispatchInput.Role is the named Role type)
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/driver.go: DispatchInput.Role is type Role (not string), consistent with RoleSet map[Role]bool

divergence.item: internal/driver/result.go (a spec touchpoint) was not created as a separate file.
divergence.reason: design.md (reviewed and PASSED without objection) proposed keeping Result-related helpers minimal and adding result.go only if a test needed one; no test needed a helper beyond the Result struct itself, which lives in driver.go alongside Driver/RoleSet/DispatchInput. Not a Rule-2 deferral (no scope was dropped — Result the type is fully delivered, AC-01) — a file-layout choice made during implementation and disclosed here per Rule 6.
